thesis: Serial entrepreneurs outperform novice entrepreneurs due to transferable learning across ventures, not selection effects.

sections.title: Introduction
sections.summary: Prior literature debates whether serial entrepreneur advantage comes from innate ability (selection) or accumulated experience (learning). This paper uses Danish register data to distinguish the two.

paragraphs.compound: Serial entrepreneurs are defined as founders of more than one firm. Approximately 10% of Danish entrepreneurs are serial, with 73% operating as portfolio entrepreneurs running concurrent ventures.
paragraphs.supporting_text: We define serial entrepreneurs as founders of more than one firm...
atoms: Serial entrepreneurs are defined as entrepreneurs who have founded more than one firm.
Approximately 10% of entrepreneurs in Denmark are serial entrepreneurs.
73% of serial entrepreneurs are portfolio entrepreneurs who run two or more businesses concurrently.
27% of serial entrepreneurs are sequential entrepreneurs.

sections.title: Results
sections.summary: Serial entrepreneurs achieve 67% higher sales than novice entrepreneurs. The advantage persists after controlling for personal characteristics and is concentrated in portfolio entrepreneurs.

paragraphs.compound: Serial entrepreneurs achieve 67% higher sales than novice entrepreneurs. This advantage is not explained by personal characteristics such as education, gender, or age.
paragraphs.supporting_text: Table 3 shows that serial entrepreneurs achieve 67% higher sales...
atoms: Serial entrepreneurs achieve 67% higher sales than novice entrepreneurs.
The serial entrepreneur sales advantage is not explained by personal characteristics such as education, gender, or age.

paragraphs.compound: Among serial entrepreneurs, 73% are portfolio type. Portfolio entrepreneur sales are 77% above novices versus only 32% for sequential entrepreneurs.
paragraphs.supporting_text: The sales gains of serial entrepreneurship are concentrated in portfolio entrepreneurs...
atoms: Portfolio entrepreneur sales are 77% above novice entrepreneur sales.
Sequential entrepreneur sales are 32% above novice entrepreneur sales.
The serial entrepreneur sales advantage is concentrated in portfolio entrepreneurs.

relationships.rationale: The 67% overall advantage is explained by concentration in portfolio entrepreneurs
